select_when: One plausible frame explains part of the problem but leaves stakeholder reactions, edge cases, or system behavior under-explained.
The goal is transfer across situations, so the team should compare examples, counterexamples, and alternative cuts of the same structure.
Leaders should examine the problem through materially different lenses before locking in the operating story.
A familiar expert pattern may be misleading and the team needs contrasted frames to expose what the first read is missing.
avoid_when: Teams are adding more frames, analogies, or examples without using them to sharpen the decision, prune hypotheses, or expose the highest-value contrast.
A simple problem already yields a clear answer and extra frame-rotation would mostly add delay, noise, or performative completeness.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on contrasting multiple frames to improve transfer and diagnosis rather than on broad multidisciplinary rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor graph-walk or ideation sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on explicit contrast before commitment.
open_questions: The source gestures toward specialization as a tension, but the current curated target surface is cleaner around anchoring and confirmation-bias.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly says the model is strongest when one frame is under-explaining the problem, when transfer matters, and when teams need contrast before commitment.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against undisciplined wandering and also notes that straightforward problems may not need heavy interleaving.

input_type.note: The source is explicit about under-explained problems and misleading first frames; the compact label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model produces contrasted frame testing and alternative cuts; the output label compresses that into a reusable artifact type.

reasoning_types.note: The model is reflective and system-aware, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
